mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.3% monthly threshold forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio over 60-day holding periods in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real gross domestic product growth directly expands aggregate corporate revenue streams, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with a classification accuracy below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals capacity constraints and rising input costs that erode profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 45% in out-of-sample testing.
